response.justification: Candidate is a strong generalist senior software developer with extensive web, full-stack, and some embedded-adjacent experience, but the role requires native-level Japanese proficiency (not listed), strong C for UNIX development as a primary skill (only briefly mentioned at National Instruments a decade ago), and specific embedded networking/security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, Linux kernel modification, switch chip SDKs) that the resume does not demonstrate. The mismatch on hard requirements—particularly the Japanese language requirement and embedded networking security background—makes this candidate unsuitable.
strengths: Senior-level experience across diverse stacks and strong software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD)
Some historical C/C++ experience at National Instruments on developer tooling
Linux familiarity and ability to read English technical documentation
concerns: No evidence of native-level Japanese language proficiency (hard requirement)
No demonstrated experience in embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, or security protocols like MACsec/802.1X
C programming and Linux kernel source modification are not core or recent skills
